story.description: A clip of dogs / puppies: a dog on leash sitting
story.hook: Dogs build a map with smell.
story.lead: Dogs build a map with smell. Watch the nose before the feet, because every sniff samples layers of scent. Dogs can learn who passed, where they moved, and what changed since the last check. The ground is basically a news feed. What should they sniff next?
story.script: Dogs build a map with smell. Watch the nose before the feet, because every sniff samples layers of scent. Dogs can learn who passed, where they moved, and what changed since the last check. The ground is basically a news feed. What should they sniff next?

story.description: A clip of cold-climate animals: a swan is swimming in the water near a boat
story.hook: Birds see colors humans cannot see.
story.lead: Birds see colors humans cannot see. Watch the feathers and eyes, because many birds see ultraviolet detail hidden from us. That extra color can help with mates, food, and navigation. The same scene is richer to them than it is to us. Which bird fact next?

story.description: A clip of farm animals: close up of two horses eating alfalfa in a pen
story.lead: Horses can remember familiar faces. Watch the eyes and ears, because horses can learn who is familiar and who feels safe. That memory changes how calmly they approach people and other horses. The look is part recognition, part decision. Which herd animal next?
story.script: Horses can remember familiar faces. Watch the eyes and ears, because horses can learn who is familiar and who feels safe. That memory changes how calmly they approach people and other horses. The look is part recognition, part decision. Which herd animal next?

story.description: A clip of farm animals: a herd of horses standing in a muddy water
story.hook: Horses show attention with their ears
story.lead: Mud isn't just dirt—watch how they coat their coats. That thick paste blocks biting flies and insulates like a winter coat. Because flies hate wet mud, that's why they do this. See their ears twitch? They're not just shaking off water—they're listening for predators while they stay protected.
story.script: Mud isn't just dirt—watch how they coat their coats. That thick paste blocks biting flies and insulates like a winter coat. Because flies hate wet mud, that's why they do this. See their ears twitch? They're not just shaking off water—they're listening for predators while they stay protected.

story.description: A clip of dogs / puppies: playful dogs interacting on a sunny day
story.hook: Dogs can follow a human point fast.
story.lead: Dogs can follow a human point fast. Watch the eyes check the person, because dogs are unusually good at using human gestures. That skill helps them cooperate with us before any command gets repeated. The small glance is social intelligence, not random obedience. What dog skill should come next?
story.script: Dogs can follow a human point fast. Watch the eyes check the person, because dogs are unusually good at using human gestures. story.description: A clip of dogs / puppies: dog looking under the table
story.hook: Dogs build a map with smell.
story.lead: Dogs build a map with smell. Watch the nose before the feet, because every sniff samples layers of scent. Dogs can learn who passed, where they moved, and what changed since the last check. The ground is basically a news feed. What should they sniff next?
story.script: Dogs build a map with smell. Watch the nose before the feet, because every sniff samples layers of scent. Dogs can learn who passed, where they moved, and what changed since the last check. The ground is basically a news feed. What should they sniff next?
